pattern: Keeping the unexplored suffix of the structure on a stack, and only unpacking a list when its turn actually arrives, turns arbitrarily deep nesting into a flat lazy stream where hasNext does the digging and next just hands over an integer.
hints: Recursively collecting every integer up front works, but the follow-up wants an iterator that does no more digging than the caller demands.
Push the top-level entries onto a stack in reverse so the leftmost sits on top; the top of the stack is always the next thing to consider.
Make hasNext do the real work: while the top is a list, pop it and push its children in reverse, discarding empties along the way — when the loop leaves an integer on top, next simply pops it.

edge_inputs.description: two nested pairs around a bare integer
empty inner lists sprinkled through the structure — hasNext must skip them without emitting anything
nothing but empty lists — hasNext is false from the start
a single bare integer at the top level
next called without interleaved hasNext probes — laziness must not depend on the caller priming it

stress.description: random nested structure (with occasional empty lists) drained with interleaved hasNext probes
